summary: This cluster focuses on the taxonomic revision and morphological characterization of the red algal family Rhodochortonaceae in Korea, with the identification of eight new species and the reclassification of thirteen existing taxa. The research centers on the genera *Acrochaetium*, *Rhodochorton*, and *Auduinella*, describing their uniseriate, branched filamentous thalli, variable chloroplast distribution, and epi- or endobiotic growth on host plants. Key findings include the description of *Acrochaetium radiatum Jao* and *Rhodochorton robustum* as new species, alongside the previously unknown *Auduinella attenituium*, highlighting the high diversity of this family in Korean coastal and benthic habitats.
